methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Exodys Energy is a Seed-stage nuclear energy company founded by Carl Perez that has raised $10M+. The valuation analysis draws from a competitive set of 11 peers (3 public, 8 private) in the nuclear energy sector, including comparable companies such as Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

A peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the nuclear energy subset—only one adjacent-stage peer was identified against a threshold requirement of three or more datapoints. Consequently, the analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 2.5x multiplier to the $10M total raised as a valuation proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $25M. This reflects typical capital step-ups observed in Seed-to-Series A advancement patterns.

The triangulated valuation range is $21M to $29M, with a median of $25M. This single-method result carries inherent uncertainty: the lack of reliable peer comparables in the narrow nuclear energy Seed-stage cohort limits cross-validation. Key sensitivities include stage-up trajectory assumptions and the degree to which broader nuclear energy peer metrics apply to an early-stage entity. Investors should consider that stage-progression mechanics may not fully capture sector-specific technological risk or the competitive positioning relative to the named peer set.
